pslq_vs_Ftheorem_ring.note: None / null relation => C_inf is NOT in the F-theorem transcendental ring

pslq_vs_Boltzmann_ring.note: relation [-1, 1, -1, -1] means -C_inf + coth1 - log2 - log(sinh1) = 0 <=> C_inf = coth(1) - log(2 sinh 1). PSLQ confirms the closed form (residual exactly 0).

note_intercept: The original a*log(n)+b fit over n=2..7 returned (a,b)=(1.963,0.540). Because the slope a was biased LOW (1.963 instead of 2) by small-n, the intercept b was biased HIGH (0.540 instead of 0.458) to compensate. Forcing slope=2 and reading the n->inf intercept gives the true constant C_inf=0.4584487... = coth(1)-log(2 sinh 1), exact.

curve_fit_audit.free_parameters: ZERO for the asymptotic claim. The slope (2) and constant (coth1 - log(2 sinh1)) are DERIVED from the closed-form degeneracy g_m=(n-m)(n-m+1) and weight e^{-(2m+1)}, not fitted. The original 2-parameter (a,b) fit is replaced by a 0-parameter derivation.
curve_fit_audit.selection_bias: None. There is no search over candidate constants -- C_inf falls out of the exact geometric-series resummation A0=1/(2 sinh1), B0/A0=coth1. PSLQ is used only to CONFIRM, not to discover.
curve_fit_audit.alternative_fits: log-quadratic had marginally higher R^2 on the small-n panel (0.999995) but the quadratic-in-log term coefficient -> 0 as the window moves up; it was over-fitting small-n curvature. The true form is exactly 2 log n + C_inf + O(1/n), single log.
curve_fit_audit.robustness: Slope converges 1.896 -> 2.0000 monotonically across n in [2,2000]; windowed least-squares slope 1.943 (n=2..7) -> 2.00005 (n=200..2000). Constant S-2logn converges to C_inf with O(1/n) corrections (coeff +0.687/n predicted, sign-correct).
curve_fit_audit.independent_cross_check: Closed-form S = log Z + <K> matched the modular_hamiltonian.py numerics to 1.8e-15 in the original sprint; the asymptotic A0, B0 geometric sums matched the finite-n panel to O(1/n).
